data.title: Seedlip partners with Dorinda Medley for flavorful drinks this Dry January, redefining modern drinking without compromise.
data.description: Today in New York, on January 9, 2026, Seedlip continues to push its mission to revolutionize the contemporary drinking scene, emphasizing that the focus should be on taste and presentation. The company aims to demonstrate that sophisticated, expertly made cocktails can be enjoyed without sacrificing quality. Seedlip, currently the leading brand in the market, is dedicated to providing a premium drinking experience.

data.title: Sienna Senior Living to report 2025 Q4 results on Feb 19, with conference call on Feb 20 at 10 am ET.
data.description: Sienna Senior Living Inc. based in Markham, Ontario, will release its financial results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on February 19, 2026, after the market closes. The company's stock is listed on the TSX under the symbol SIA. data.title: US economy adds 50,000 jobs in December; unemployment rate falls to 4.4%. Wage growth accelerates.
data.description: An updated report from the latest U.S. labor market data revealed that the U.S. economy concluded 2025 with a more stable labor market than anticipated, alleviating concerns over escalating job losses in the late summer and early fourth quarter. In December, nonfarm payrolls saw an increase of 50,000 jobs, a slight deviation from November's revised gain of 56,000, and slightly below the projected 60,000 growth. The Bureau of Labor Statistics disclosed that October's payrolls were adjusted down by 68,000, showcasing deeper job losses from 105,000 to 173,000. The unemployment rate decreased from a revised 4.5% in November to 4.4%, falling under the expected 4.5%. Additionally, wage growth showed improvement as average hourly earnings rose by 0.3% in December, outpacing the 0.2% increase in November and aligning with predictions. The full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Jamf named Leader in 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Endpoint Management Tools, only Apple-focused platform.
data.description: Jamf, a company listed on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol JAMF, headquartered in Minneapolis, has been acknowledged as a Leader in the 2026 Gartner Magic Quadrant for Endpoint Management Tools. This recognition is based on criteria evaluating Completeness of Vision and Ability to Execute. Jamf stands out as the sole Apple-centric platform offering a comprehensive suite of management, security, and AI-driven automation solutions. This enables organizations to benefit from native Apple integration, top-tier security measures, and effortless compatibility with their current systems.

data.title: BigBear.ai partners with Kraft Group and New England Patriots for digital transformation, vision alignment between organizations.
data.description: BigBear.ai, a prominent provider of AI solutions for national security and complex enterprises, has unveiled a new collaboration with the Kraft Group. This partnership involves the Kraft family's diverse businesses, encompassing global operations in sports, paper and packaging manufacturing, and forest products distribution. BigBear.ai, listed as BBAI on the NYSE, and the Kraft Group have joined forces based on a shared vision.

data.title: Polestar 2025: 15,608 vehicles sold in Q4 (27% increase), total 60,119 in year (34% rise). CEO: 'Best year ever' despite challenges.
data.description: Polestar Automotive (NASDAQ: PSNY) reported a strong fourth quarter in 2025 with an estimated retail sales of 15,608 vehicles, reflecting a notable 27% increase from the same period in 2024. Throughout the entire year, Polestar achieved approximately 60,119 retail sales, marking a significant 34% surge compared to the previous year's 44,851 vehicles. CEO Michael Lohscheller attributed this success to the company's resilience in the face of external challenges and tough market conditions, declaring 2025 as Polestar's best year in terms of retail sales. This positive performance has seen Polestar gaining market share and surpassing many established competitors in the automotive industry.

data.title: Dow Jones rose after surprise jobs report. Trump tariff ruling pending. Oklo stock soared.
data.description: On Friday, the Dow Jones index experienced an increase following an unexpected rise in the jobs report. The ruling on Trump's tariffs is eagerly awaited. Oklo also saw a significant surge. This update on the stock market today highlights the Dow's positive movement after the surprising job data, while the impending decision on Trump's tariffs continues to impact market sentiments.

data.title: Dow Jones futures increase ahead of jobs report and Trump tariff ruling. Oklo, Revolution Medicines see early success.
data.description: The upcoming December jobs report and a potential ruling on tariffs by Trump are anticipated. Early winners include Oklo, Vistra, and Revolution Medicines. Before the release of the data, Dow Jones futures are on the rise, with Oklo and Revolution Medicines seeing significant increases.

data.title: Bitcoin exchange reserves are declining to 2.4-2.8 million BTC (from 3 million in 2020) as holders opt for long-term storage. This shift is reshaping market dynamics with increased off-exchange activity and institutional investors moving away from exchanges.
data.description: Bitcoin is experiencing a significant decrease in the amount held on centralized exchanges, dropping to an estimated range of 2.4 million to 2.8 million BTC as of early January 2026, down from over 3 million BTC in 2020. This shift indicates a trend of investors moving towards long-term storage solutions, with platforms like Coinbase, Binance, and Kraken seeing continued reductions in BTC balances. With fewer coins available on exchanges, market liquidity dynamics are changing, with off-exchange activity playing a larger role in shaping buyer-seller balances. Factors contributing to this trend include increased adoption of cold storage and hardware wallets, along with the expansion of Bitcoin exchange-traded funds. Institutional and retail investors are increasingly comfortable holding Bitcoin outside of exchange environments, indicating a shift in how the asset is traded and valued.

data.title: Office leasing activity up 5% in 2025, Boston top growth market. 410M square feet leased. Lowest level in 15 years in 2024.
data.description: In the U.S., office leasing activity experienced a 5% year-over-year increase, as per data from CoStar. The city of Boston saw the highest growth in this metric. According to CoStar's year-end report, office tenants leased around 410 million square feet of space in 2025. This marked a rise from the previous year, in which leasing volume hit its lowest point in 15 years, excluding the impact of 2020.
